background: We have established a scenario in which four patients are treated with an intervention that is successful 75% of the time. The number of patients who respond to treatment is a binomial random variable with parameters n = 4 and p = 0.75. It follows that probability of a failure = 1 - p = 1 - 0.75 = 0.25.

task.query: What is the probability of observing no successes among the four treatments?
What is the probability of one success?
What is the probability of two successes?
What is the probability of three successes?
What is the probability of four successes?
What is the probability mass function (pmf) for X ~ b(4, 0.75) in tabular form?
task.answer:
Table 6.1 lists this pmf in tabular form:

\begin{tabular}{lccccc}
\hline \multicolumn{6}{l}{ Table 6.1. Probability mass function for $X \sim b(4,0.75)$ in tabular form. } \
\hline No. of successes $(x)$ & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 \
$\operatorname{Pr}(X=x)$ & 0.0039 & 0.0469 & 0.2109 & 0.4219 & 0.3164 \
\hline
\end{tabular}
